English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Forest Hills Gardens Corporation records
Forest Hills Gardens Corporation (Forest Hills, New York, N.Y.)
2775
29.4 cubic feet. 29.4 cubic feet.
1922-1979,-1922-1958 (bulk) Correspondence, minutes, reports, memoranda, vouchers, orders, notices, clippings, specifications, and other materials. Collection includes materials relating to the history of Forest Hills Gardens, a planned residential community in Queens, New York that was devleloped by the Russel Sage Foundation in 1908. Collection material in English 2067637
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
R. Esakof
Date completed:
August 1987
EAD encoding:
Ann Hubert April-July 2002Martin Heggestad
Date modified:
Jude Corina, July 2017
ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
Forest Hills Gardens is a planned residential community which was developed by the Russell Sage Foundation in 1908. Laid out on 175 acres in Queens, New York, the community's site plan was designed by Frederick Law Olmsted; many of the houses were designed by Grosvenor Atterbury.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Collection includes correspondence, minutes, reports, memoranda, vouchers, orders, notices, clippings, specifications, and other materials pertaining to Forest Hills Gardens meetings, committees, director, constitution and by-laws, insurance and liability, finance, maintenance, development, construction, zoning, maintaining architectural standards and aesthetic control, building restrictions and waivers, streets and parking, complaints, policies, the sale of property to "undesirables," the Ives-Quinn anti-discrimination bill, and other subjects. Also included are reports of the Architectural Committee and correspondence with the Russell Sage Foundation, the Sage Foundation Homes Co., Forest Hills Dwellings, Inc., residents, and the New York City Planning Commission.
RELATED MATERIAL
See also Forest Hills Gardens (New York, N.Y.) Records, ( #2495).
General
Development of the Vanderveer Tract
Materials in Box 28, where noted, pertain to the development of the Vanderveer Tract, which adjoins Forest Hills Gardens and over which FHG has architectural control as per agreement of May 17, 1923
SERIES LIST
Series I. Alphabetical Files and Other Correspondence
1921-1922 1923-1929 1930-1934 1935-1939 1940-1944 1945-1950 Miscellaneous Vanderveer Tract Block 12
Series II. Vouchers, Permits, and Legal Documents
Vouchers Permits Legal Documents
Series III. Maps, Plans, Oversized Items, Brochures, and Glass Plate Negatives
Maps, Plans, Oversized Items Brochures Glass Plate Negatives
Available Copies:
Corporate minutes, 1922-1958 are on microfilm. Originals retained by donor.
Cite As:
Forest Hills Gardens Corporation Records, #2775. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Access Restrictions:
Access to microfilm of corporate minutes restricted to the permission of the donor.

Scope and Contents
A collection of early views of Forest Hills Gardens, 1913 to approximately 1920, primarily of the Forest Hills Inn, Station Square and Flag-pole Green at the time of the July 4th celebrations. Observance of this Holiday was very important and was a day long event beginning with a sunrise proclamation by a Town-crier dress in costume (see photo) and continuing with a parade, games, dances, speeches, and a flag raising ceremony at Flag-pole Green
The Perry Collection #3442, 7-74 case 3, has a box with a collection of 37 lantern slides used by Mr. Perry for lectures on city planning Aug. 1, 1946. Fourteen of these slides are of Forest Hills Gardens, showing early views of Station Square dated July 1914 and of the July 4, 1914 celebration (similar to #28-41 in Box 30, #2775). Also, included is an early landscape overview of the Terraces (and Block 12) looking east from the Forest Hills Inn and an early view of the Olivia Park. A slide of an entrance gate to the Gardens is also included1 - The town crier302-27 - Spectators, games, races, flag raising ceremonies in Flag-pole Green on occasion of 4th of July celebration earlier - some Atterbury row houses visible on Greenway Terrances19203028-41 - July 4th celebration observances in Station Square and Forest Hills Inn area - speaker shown in #28 and #29, may be Teddy Roosevelt, a frequent visitor to Forest Hills Gardens19203042-72 - Young dancing girls performing outdoors in July 4th celebration events19203073 - B of 4 section Panorama3074-76 - Early view Station Square19133077 - View from Burns Street towards Square19303078 - Early view from Terraces into Station Square ca.19133079 - Station Square19203080 - Station Square (Christmas tree in Square)19203081-82 - Station Square19203083 - Detail of street sign3084 - Street lantern affixed to Forest Hills Inn in Station Square area3085 - Lantern light fixture near Forest Hills Inn in Station Square area3086 - Greenway Terrace group of row houses (Atterbury)19203087 - Early housepre-19203088 - Early housepre-19203089 - Early house3090 - Early homes under constructionpre-19203091 - Atterbury house corner of Greenway North and Overhill Road3092 - Early grandstands at West Side Tennis Club before tennis stadium was built3093 - Early photo of West Side Tennis Club before the stadium was built3094 - Playground3095 - Pathway and trees3096 - Dogwood treesMay 19133097 - Undeveloped property3098 - Unidentified30 ======= Alphabetical Files

Scope and Contents
A collection of early views of Forest Hills Gardens, 1913 to approximately 1920, primarily of the Forest Hills Inn, Station Square and Flag-pole Green at the time of the July 4th celebrations. Observance of this Holiday was very important and was a day long event beginning with a sunrise proclamation by a Town-crier dress in costume (see photo) and continuing with a parade, games, dances, speeches, and a flag raising ceremony at Flag-pole Green
The Perry Collection #3442, 7-74 case 3, has a box with a collection of 37 lantern slides used by Mr. Perry for lectures on city planning Aug. 1, 1946. Fourteen of these slides are of Forest Hills Gardens, showing early views of Station Square dated July 1914 and of the July 4, 1914 celebration (similar to #28-41 in Box 30, #2775). Also, included is an early landscape overview of the Terraces (and Block 12) looking east from the Forest Hills Inn and an early view of the Olivia Park.
